title: Gensler: Big Deals a Sign of 'High-Valuation Market'
summary: Gary Gensler, Former SEC Chair and professor at MIT Sloan School of Management, joins Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on Bloomberg's 'The Close' and discusses the significant return of IPO activity including SK Hynix, which raised $26.5 billion in the largest foreign debut in the US, Google's $85 billion capital raise and SpaceX going public. He says these big deals are a sign of 'high-valuation market.'  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bloomberg Money 7/10/2026
summary: Bloomberg Money takes the pulse of your financial life, powered by the reporting of our global newsroom. Today's guests are: University of Chicago Booth School Professor of Economics and Former Fed Governor Randall Kroszner and NewEdge Wealth CIO Cameron Dawson. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SK Hynix Debut Is a Bet That AI Breaks Boom-and-Bust Chip Cycle
summary: South Korean memory chipmaker SK Hynix Inc. just pulled off the largest public listing by a foreign company in US market history. Shares soared 13% on their first day of trading.

title: Rhode Island Jail Operator Files Bankruptcy to Cut Bond Debt
summary: Central Falls Detention Facility Corp., which houses federal detainees including people arrested by the US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency, filed for bankruptcy saying it cannot afford to keep paying on more than $167 million in bond debt.

title: Wheat Soars as Ukraine Attacks on Russia Seen as Export Risk
summary: Wheat prices jumped Friday as traders weighed potential shipping disruptions following Ukraine’s attacks on top exporter Russia.

title: Insuring Rare and Collectible Cars and Boats: Masters in Business with McKeel Hagerty
summary: Barry sits down with with CEO and Chairman of Hagerty, McKeel Hagerty. They discuss how he transformed the the family boat insurance business into a "sexy" driver forward business. Barry and McKeel also discuss their love of collectable cars and his love of his first car, a Porsche, that he bought at the age of 13. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: A Hidden-Debt Scandal Derails Senegal's Infrastructure Dream
summary: Unfinished railway is emblematic of the country’s fiscal strains.

title: The Grueling Economics of Tennis
summary: Some of the world’s top tennis players will face off at the Wimbledon finals this weekend. The players who make it to Centre Court have already achieved a bigger feat: a career as a tennis player. On today’s Big Take podcast, Bloomberg’s Hannah Miller and The Athletic’s Matt Futterman break down what sets tennis apart from other sports and the challenges the tournaments and players face to stay profitable. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Seeing Signs of AI Fatigue: CreditSights Global Head of Strategy
summary: CreditSights Global Head of Strategy Winnie Cisar discusses the tech sector and what to expect from AI. She speaks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Meta Shares Gain Most Since April 2025 | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Isabelle Lee. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gotham FC Announces Move to Etihad Stadium in 2028
summary: Gotham FC governor Carolyn Tisch-Blodgett is joined by Bloomberg's Vanessa Perdomo-Maglione to discuss the NWSL club's historic move to Etihad Stadium in Queens in 2028. Tisch-Blodgett said that its the 'duty' of the league and every team to take excitement from global events like the World Cup and translate it into 'lifelong fandom.' (Source: Bloomberg)

title: More Americans Are Getting Prenups, Even If They Aren't Rich
summary: Bloomberg's Nikki Waller joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." Belle Burden’s blockbuster memoir, 'Strangers,' has riveted readers with the tale of her marriage’s sudden implosion and aftermath. They also discuss the rising popularity of prenups has been driven by changing financial dynamics of marriage. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stock Futures Slip After Tech Rally, SK Hynix Starts Trading
summary: US equities posted gains for a second consecutive week, even as investors digested the record US listing of South Korea’s SK Hynix Inc. and renewed tensions in Iran ahead of second-quarter earnings.

title: Hit Memoir "Strangers" Spurs Marital Money Rethink
summary:
Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene discuss their must-read book on this weeks edition of "Bloomberg Money." Belle Burden’s blockbuster memoir, "Strangers," has riveted readers with the tale of her marriage’s sudden implosion and aftermath.  It has also spurred some of them to open the books on their marital finances.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Echoes the Dot-Com Market Split as Iran Threat Creeps Back
summary: It was a split-screen week on Wall Street.

title: 'Moana,' Rolling Stones, Retail Spending: Look Ahead
summary: Bloomberg's Lisa Mateo joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." They discuss the new released live action Moana movie, the new Rolling Stones album "Foreign Tongues," June CPI, PPI, and retail sails. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: S&P 500 Rises Before Earnings as SK Hynix Debuts: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street traders drove stocks higher in the countdown to the earnings season, with SK Hynix Inc. jumping in its debut and the market parsing the latest geopolitical developments.

title: Youth Sports Cost Debate Rekindled After US Soccer Exit
summary: Bloomberg's Randall Williams joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." They discuss the growing cost of youth sports for American families, which has become a $40 billion-a-year private industry. The high cost is listed as a factor in why US Soccer can't take the next step on the world stage. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Heads for Weekly Loss as Traders Consider Iran, Rates Path
summary: Gold headed for a weekly loss as investors assessed renewed fighting in the Middle East and the prospects for interest-rate hikes to combat inflation.

title: Households All-In On Equities: Cameron Dawson
summary:
Cameron Dawson, CEO of NewEdge Wealth, joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." They discuss financial planning and allocating personal portfolios.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ING Weighs Venezuela Commodity Deals as Client Requests Rise
summary: ING Groep, one of the top financiers of commodity trading, is fielding “a lot” of calls from clients to support Venezuelan natural resource deals as the country revamps exports after years of US sanctions, its new Americas commodity finance head said.

title: Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Over But Talks on Deal to Continue
summary:
President Donald Trump said the US would continue talks with Iran but considers the ceasefire between the countries to be over.
“The Islamic Republic of Iran has asked us to continue ‘talks.’ We have agreed to do so, but the United States has stated to them, in no uncertain terms, that the Cease Fire is OVER!,” Trump said Friday on social media. Trita Parsi, Executive Vice President at the Quincy Institute for Responsible Statecraft joined to discuss also saying the track record of the US in the region, particularly in terms of democratizing countries, is abysmal. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Eases as US, Iran Continue Talks After Renewed Fighting
summary: Oil prices slipped as the US and Iran appear set to continue talks following a flare-up in attacks that strained their ceasefire and deterred tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Exxon Supports SEC Proposal to End Mandatory Quarterly Reports
summary: ExxonMobil Holdings Corp. supports a US Securities and Exchange Commission proposal to replace quarterly financial reporting with semiannual disclosures, bucking the trend among most respondents the regulator’s request for comments.
